profiles.name: small_task
profiles.description: Quick dev cycle for a small, well-scoped change — plan → implement, no QA loops. The cheapest work kind, intended for small direct edits. No cross_gates block → no terminal cross gates run; ship-readiness here is the operator's call.

profiles.name: feature
profiles.description: Production-grade dev cycle for a feature: plan ↔ validate_plan loop, subtask_dag implement, review_changes ↔ repair_changes loop, final_acceptance. The default work kind for shipped work. validate_plan uses the plan_reviewer persona; review_changes uses code_reviewer; implement↔repair_changes share the implementation_engineer role thread. Reviewer and implementation phases use per_role boundaries; plan and final_acceptance stay common. Opts into both terminal cross gates strictly: contract_check (cross-contract review) and cross_final_acceptance (system release gate), both run=always with on_skip=block.

profiles.name: complex_feature
profiles.description: More comprehensive development cycle for complex work + extension-point compliance/audit gate (no-op stub in core; embedders register their own handler via the orcho.phases entry_points group). Opts into both terminal cross gates strictly (same policy as feature). validate_plan uses the plan_reviewer persona; review_changes uses code_reviewer; implement↔repair_changes share the implementation_engineer role thread. Reviewer and implementation phases use per_role boundaries; plan, final_acceptance, and the role-less compliance_check gate stay common. Runtime context lifecycle is runtime-owned per ADR 0030; contract recovery handles broken contracts from auto-compaction.

profiles.name: planning
profiles.description: Produce a plan artifact and stop for human review. validate_plan carries handoff: human_feedback_always so every verdict (approved or rejected) pauses with status awaiting_phase_handoff; the human decides continue / retry_feedback / halt via the SDK. Useful for review-driven workflows — downstream implement / review_changes are the user's call (or a follow-up `task` profile after the decision lands). No cross_gates block → no terminal cross gates run (a planning-only run has nothing for them to review).

profiles.name: research
profiles.description: Investigate and produce a plan artifact, then stop for human review — same plan-only recipe as planning, reused for exploratory research work with no delivery gates by default. validate_plan carries handoff: human_feedback_always so every verdict pauses for the human to decide continue / retry_feedback / halt via the SDK. No cross_gates block → no terminal cross gates run.

profiles.name: delivery_audit
profiles.description: Audit uncommitted changes for delivery readiness — review_changes pass over the current working tree (skips on no-uncommitted) then a final_acceptance verdict. For pre-commit / pre-PR sanity checks.

profiles.name: code_review
profiles.description: Review uncommitted changes — same review_changes → final_acceptance recipe as delivery_audit, reused for a code-review focused pass over the operator's current working tree. For pre-commit / pre-PR review.

profiles.name: refactor
profiles.description: Production-grade dev cycle for a refactor — same full_cycle recipe as feature (plan ↔ validate_plan loop, subtask_dag implement, review_changes ↔ repair_changes loop, final_acceptance), reused for restructuring work. Reviewer and implementation phases use per_role boundaries; plan and final_acceptance stay common. Opts into both terminal cross gates strictly: contract_check and cross_final_acceptance, both run=always with on_skip=block.

profiles.name: migration
profiles.description: More comprehensive development cycle for a migration — same recipe as complex_feature (full_cycle + extension-point compliance/audit gate; no-op stub in core, embedders register their own handler via orcho.phases). Opts into both terminal cross gates strictly. validate_plan uses the plan_reviewer persona; review_changes uses code_reviewer; implement↔repair_changes share the implementation_engineer role thread. Reviewer and implementation phases use per_role boundaries; plan, final_acceptance, and the role-less compliance_check gate stay common.

profiles.name: task
profiles.description: Internal profile (not a public work kind). Implement against an existing plan: skips planning, runs implement + review_changes/repair_changes loop + final_acceptance. The plan must already exist (e.g. produced by a prior `planning` run). Selective per_role on the implementation_engineer pair (implement ↔ repair_changes) so the engineer thread shards its context across the repair loop; solo-role steps (review_changes — only reviewer in this scoped profile, final_acceptance) stay common.

profiles.name: correction
profiles.description: Internal/system profile (ADR 0085) for the correction follow-up after a rejected final_acceptance — NOT a first-run profile. The interactive fresh-run picker hides it; without correction context (no correction_context.md and no follow-up parent) the opening correction_triage phase fail-fast halts the run. Starts at correction_triage (read-only classification of the recorded release blockers), then implement → review_changes/repair_changes loop → final_acceptance. No plan / validate_plan: the change already has a plan from the parent run and the retained worktree is the subject of the correction. Never projected to cross — declares no cross annotations or cross_gates.

selectors.name: auto-detect
selectors.description: Semantic selector (non-executable): core classifies the work kind and selects the matching semantic profile + mode. Pass as the ``profile`` argument to orcho_run_start.
